alert_overflow_details: Event query returned {raw} rows (cap {cap}) between {since} and {until}. The PCE sync events API returns only the newest rows, so older events in this window were permanently missed. Consider narrowing the poll interval or reviewing burst sources.
alert_traffic_overflow_details: Traffic ingest hit the {cap}-row cap ({raw} rows returned) in window {since} to {until} and could not bisect the window any further. Flow data for that window may be incomplete — some flows were permanently dropped from the cache. Consider raising traffic_sampling.max_rows_per_batch or reviewing burst sources.

alert_note_event: This section lists recent high-value events and operational context to help decide whether to investigate accounts, Workloads, or Policy changes.
alert_note_health: This section summarises PCE or Cluster health anomalies — review first to confirm whether connectivity, control plane, or service availability is affected.
alert_note_metric: This section aggregates high-bandwidth or high-metric anomalies — useful for spotting peak behaviour, traffic amplification, or resource imbalance.
alert_note_traffic: This section summarises anomalous traffic and representative connections so you can read the risk shape from conditions, hot connections, and snapshot data.

rpt_guidance_mod_labels_actions: Assign the missing Label keys on listed Workloads; resolve conflicting label sets at the source system; re-run the report to confirm coverage improves.
rpt_guidance_mod_labels_how: Coverage counts Workloads carrying all four keys (app/env/loc/role). Flow gap counts flows where a managed endpoint has no App Label. Conflicts list IPs observed with more than one label set.
rpt_guidance_mod_labels_purpose: Measure labeling quality — incomplete or conflicting Labels make Policy unreliable and block enforcement progress.
rpt_guidance_mod_labels_signals: Coverage below 90%; any conflict rows; a rising flow gap after onboarding new Workloads.
rpt_labels_no_inventory: Workload inventory unavailable (CSV source or API error) — showing flow-derived metrics only.
rpt_tr_sec_labels_intro: Measure Label coverage and conflicts — labeling quality determines Policy quality.

event_tips_agent_activate: VEN successfully paired with PCE and is now managed. Pair with agent.deactivate to track workload lifecycle. Filter by status=success or status=failure to detect pairing failures.
event_tips_agent_clone_detected: PCE detected two VENs with duplicate identities (cloned host scenario). Immediate investigation required — this can indicate unauthorized duplication. Pair with agent.activate_clone to see the clone activation.
event_tips_agent_deactivate: VEN removed from PCE management (unpair or decommission). Pair with agent.activate for lifecycle tracking and workloads.unpair for the workload-level event that usually accompanies deactivation.
event_tips_agent_goodbye: VEN-initiated disconnect before shutdown or restart. Pair with agent.service_not_available to distinguish clean shutdowns from failures. Absence of goodbye before a service_not_available event indicates an unclean failure.
event_tips_agent_refresh_policy: VEN fetched a fresh copy of its policy from PCE. High-frequency during provisioning; suppress with a severity filter in steady-state. No sub-variants.
event_tips_agent_service_not_available: VEN became unreachable (crash, host down, network cut). Watch alongside agent.goodbye — if goodbye did not precede this event, the failure was unclean. No sub-variants.
event_tips_agent_suspend: Enforcement paused on the workload; policy rules no longer applied. A workload in suspended state accepts all traffic — alert on this event. Check agent.activate and agent.deactivate for lifecycle context.
event_tips_agent_tampering: VEN binary or config file was modified outside of approved channels — an integrity violation. High-priority alert; this is a security incident indicator. No sub-variants; no status/severity filter needed.
event_tips_all: Matches every event regardless of type. Best used as a catch-all or for testing. Prefer specific event types to reduce noise; filter by category or actor for targeted monitoring.
event_tips_api_key_create: A new API key was issued. High-sensitivity event — unauthorized key creation grants persistent API access. Pair with api_key.delete for key lifecycle. Filter by actor to track who issued keys.
event_tips_api_key_delete: An API key was revoked. Pair with api_key.create for full key lifecycle. Unexpected deletions (especially of service account keys) may indicate account takeover or sabotage.
event_tips_cluster_update: PCE cluster configuration was modified. Rare in steady-state — alert on unexpected changes. No sub-variants. Use an actor advanced matcher to track who made the change.
event_tips_enforcement_boundary_create: An Enforcement Boundary (Selective Enforcement deny rule) was created. Affects all workloads in scope after provisioning. Alert on unexpected creation. Pair with enforcement_boundary.delete for lifecycle tracking.
event_tips_enforcement_boundary_delete: An Enforcement Boundary was removed. Removing a boundary widens selective enforcement coverage. Alert on deletions in production environments. Pair with enforcement_boundary.create for lifecycle.
event_tips_firewall_settings_update: Global firewall settings were changed (e.g., logging mode, default deny rules). High-impact event — changes apply system-wide. No sub-variants or related lifecycle events. Alert on any unexpected change.
event_tips_lost_agent_found: A VEN that was previously unreachable has reconnected. Often follows agent.service_not_available. Use to close incidents. Alert severity: informational (recovery event). No sub-variants.
event_tips_nen_degraded: NEN is in a degraded state and may not be enforcing policy correctly — an escalation of network_enforcement_node.missed_heartbeats. Immediate action required; network devices may operate without up-to-date policy.
event_tips_nen_missed_heartbeats: NEN missed expected heartbeats — early warning before full degradation. Pair with network_enforcement_node.degraded for progression tracking. Alert early to preempt policy enforcement failures on network devices.
event_tips_request_authentication_failed: API or UI authentication attempt rejected (invalid credentials, expired token, missing API key). Filter by actor IP for brute-force detection. Pair with user.authenticate for the full authentication picture.
event_tips_request_authorization_failed: Authenticated user attempted to access a resource beyond their role permissions. Alert on repeated failures — may indicate privilege escalation attempts. No sub-variants.
event_tips_rule_set_create: A new Rule Set was created. Changes take effect only after sec_policy.create (provisioning). Pair with rule_set.update and rule_set.delete to track the full policy lifecycle. Monitor actor for unauthorized policy authoring.
event_tips_rule_set_delete: A Rule Set was deleted — irreversible once provisioned. Alert on unexpected deletions; this removes a logical grouping of security rules. Pair with rule_set.create and rule_set.update for full lifecycle.
event_tips_rule_set_update: An existing Rule Set was modified. Changes are draft until provisioned via sec_policy.create. Pair with rule_set.create and rule_set.delete. Use status=failure to catch failed updates.
event_tips_sec_policy_create: A new policy version was provisioned and pushed to all VENs — the 'commit' event. All preceding rule and rule-set changes take effect here. Alert on unexpected provisioning (off-hours, unknown actor). No sub-variants.
event_tips_sec_rule_create: An individual security rule was created within a Rule Set. Draft until provisioned. Pair with sec_rule.update and sec_rule.delete, and sec_policy.create for the provisioning trigger.
event_tips_sec_rule_delete: An individual security rule was deleted. Deleting rules widens allowed traffic once provisioned. Always pair with a corresponding sec_policy.create watch to know when deletions take effect.
event_tips_sec_rule_update: An existing security rule was modified. Draft until provisioned. Pair with sec_rule.create and sec_rule.delete. Use status=failure to detect authoring errors or permission issues.
event_tips_system_task_agent_missed_hear: PCE's scheduled check found VENs that missed expected heartbeats — a leading indicator before full disconnection. Watch alongside system_task.agent_offline_check to track progression from missed to offline.
event_tips_system_task_agent_offline_che: PCE's scheduled check found VENs confirmed offline. More definitive than system_task.agent_missed_heartbeats_check. Alert here for SLA-impacting incidents; use missed_heartbeats for early warning.
event_tips_user_authenticate: Covers all authentication attempts (success and failure). Filter by status=failure to detect brute force. Pairs with user.sign_in (fires only on success) and request.authentication_failed (API-level failures).
event_tips_user_login_session_terminated: Session ended by timeout or admin revocation — not a voluntary sign-out. Alert on admin-revoked sessions for security visibility. Pair with user.sign_out to distinguish voluntary vs forced termination.
event_tips_user_pce_session_terminated: PCE server terminated the session (e.g., PCE restart, maintenance). Different from user-initiated sign-out. No sub-variants. Monitor during maintenance windows or unexpected PCE restarts.
event_tips_user_sign_in: Fires only on successful console/API sign-in. For failed attempts use user.authenticate with status=failure or request.authentication_failed. Pair with user.sign_out and user.login_session_terminated for full session tracking.
event_tips_user_sign_out: User explicitly signed out of PCE. Pair with user.sign_in for session lifecycle. Distinguish from user.login_session_terminated (timeout/admin revoke) and user.pce_session_terminated (server-side termination).
event_tips_workloads_unpair: One or more workloads were unmanaged from PCE (VEN removed). Usually accompanies agent.deactivate. Alert on bulk unpairing (high count) — may indicate a configuration error or unauthorized decommission.

gui_cache_ports_help: Restrict ingestion to these destination port numbers (comma-separated). Leave blank for all ports.
gui_cache_rate_limit_per_minute_help: Maximum PCE API calls per minute across all ingestors. Prevents rate-limit errors on busy PCEs. Recommended: 60–120.
gui_cache_traffic_agg_retention_days_hel: Days of aggregated daily traffic summaries to keep. Used for reports and trend charts.
gui_cache_traffic_poll_interval_seconds_: How often (in seconds) to fetch new traffic flows from the PCE. Traffic data is typically polled less frequently than events.
gui_cache_traffic_raw_retention_days_hel: Days of raw per-flow traffic records to keep. These are used for detailed SIEM forwarding.
gui_cache_ts_max_rows_help: Maximum number of traffic flow records fetched from the PCE per ingest cycle. Lower this if the PCE API times out or memory usage is too high.
gui_cache_ts_ratio_help: 1 = keep every Allowed flow. N = keep 1 in every N Allowed flows (e.g. 10 = 10% sample). Blocked and Potentially Blocked flows are always kept in full. Sampling uses a deterministic hash so the same flow is consistently included or excluded across cycles.

gui_card_unknown_types_tooltip: PCE event types that arrived but matched no configured monitoring rule. Capped at 100 distinct types. A non-zero value means your rules may be missing coverage for new event categories; review them in the Events tab.

Cooldown silences repeats for N minutes after each alert — this also acts as the per-rule rate cap (effectively 1 alert per cooldown period). For a higher tolerance (e.g. 2 alerts per 15 min), halve the cooldown to ~7 min instead.

rpt_au_draft_change_detail_note: The change_detail summary aggregates field-level before/after differences for Draft edits, so auditors can verify scope and intent without opening raw JSON.
rpt_au_draft_desc: These events show policy edits that have not yet taken effect.
rpt_au_draft_subnote: Draft changes are edits that have not been provisioned yet. Before the next Provision, review target, resource, action, and change detail to confirm scope.
rpt_au_event_agent_clone_detected_rec: Identify clone source; revoke and re-pair affected VEN
rpt_au_event_agent_suspend_rec: Verify if suspension was authorized; review PCE audit trail
rpt_au_event_agent_tampering_rec: Investigate workload for compromise; review iptables changes
rpt_au_event_agents_unpair_rec: Verify planned maintenance; check who initiated unpair
rpt_au_event_api_key_create_rec: Confirm with admin; revoke if unauthorized
rpt_au_event_api_key_delete_rec: Confirm with admin if deletion was intended
rpt_au_event_authentication_settings_upd: Verify MFA or session settings were not weakened
rpt_au_event_lost_agent_found_rec: Review what policies were missing during outage
rpt_au_event_lost_agent_found_summary: Lost VEN reconnected — policy gap existed
rpt_au_event_request_authorization_faile: Review user/key attempting access; check RBAC assignments
API authorization failure — privilege escalation attempt
rpt_au_event_rule_set_create_rec: Verify change was authorized; review scope
rpt_au_event_rule_set_update_rec: Verify change was authorized; review scope
rpt_au_event_sec_policy_create_rec: Review workloads_affected count; verify change was intentional
rpt_au_event_sec_rule_create_rec: Verify change was authorized; check resource_changes
rpt_au_event_sec_rule_update_rec: Verify change was authorized; check before/after diff
rpt_au_event_system_task_agent_missed_he: Check network to PCE; verify VEN service is running
rpt_au_event_user_use_expired_password_r: Prompt user to reset password immediately
rpt_au_event_workloads_unpair_rec: Verify planned maintenance; check who initiated unpair
rpt_au_failed_detail_desc: Includes source IP and notification context for suspicious login investigation.
rpt_au_failed_detail_subnote: Failed login records include the parsed target user, source IP, supplied username, and action path. Cross-check by user or source IP to spot repeated-failure patterns.
rpt_au_high_impact_desc: The following provision events affected an unusually large number of workloads.
rpt_au_lifecycle_draft_body: Draft events represent policy edits that have not yet been provisioned to VENs.
rpt_au_lifecycle_prov_body: Provision events package draft changes into a new active policy version and push them to workloads.
rpt_au_lifecycle_title: Illumio Policy Lifecycle: Draft vs Provision
rpt_au_mod01_intro: This section covers platform health, agent connectivity issues, and host-level security events. Cross-reference actor, target, source IP, and parser notes to decide if an event needs deeper investigation.
rpt_au_mod02_intro: User activity analysis focuses on parsed principal and action. The report prefers the affected user account as the primary identity, falling back to the actor field when the target cannot be resolved.
rpt_au_mod03_intro: Policy events now expose the parsed actor, target, resource, action, and change summary together, making it easier to separate Draft edits from Provision operations that actually affect Workloads.
rpt_au_mod04_intro: This section analyses temporal correlation between events, detecting typical attack-chain patterns such as Policy changes after auth failures, brute-force attempts, and high-risk off-hours operations.
rpt_au_offhours_desc: High-risk policy changes outside business hours (08:00–19:00 UTC). These warrant verification with the actor.
rpt_au_per_user_policy_subnote: This table aggregates Policy activity by parsed actor, separating admin operations, system tasks, and actions initiated by the Agent itself.
rpt_au_provision_change_detail_note: The change_detail summary shows commit messages, versions, and counts of changed and affected resources for Provision events.
rpt_au_provision_desc: Provision events push draft changes into active enforcement. Review the impact carefully.
rpt_au_provision_subnote: Provision records show deployment impact directly. Cross-reference workloads affected, actor, source IP, resource name, and change detail to confirm that large Policy changes are expected.
rpt_au_recent: Recent Events (top 10 — full list in CSV/XLSX)
rpt_au_sec_concern_desc: Tampering, suspend, or clone-related events may indicate compromise and should be reviewed immediately.
rpt_au_src_ip_note: Source IP Tracking: Unexpected source IPs may indicate compromised credentials or insider threats.
rpt_cat_bandwidth_desc: Large transfer patterns that may indicate staging or exfiltration.
rpt_cat_lateralmovement_desc: Patterns indicating pivoting, discovery, identity abuse, or cross-segment expansion.
rpt_cat_ransomware_desc: Traffic patterns related to ransomware propagation and remote control.
rpt_cat_unmanagedhost_desc: Risks introduced by hosts operating outside VEN enforcement.
rpt_cat_useractivity_desc: Suspicious user behavior and authentication anomalies.
rpt_change_impact_no_previous: No previous snapshot — change impact will appear on the next report run.
rpt_draft_actions_aab_verify_intent: Confirm boundary crossing was intentional; otherwise add boundary deny or scope rule.
rpt_draft_actions_remediate_override_den: Review the active allow rule and the override-deny boundary; tighten or remove conflict.
rpt_drift_disappeared: Disappeared Pairs (seen last period, absent now)
rpt_drift_first_run: No previous flow baseline — drift will appear from the next report onward.
rpt_drift_incomparable: Baseline window differs materially from this run ({prev} days vs {curr} days) — drift comparison skipped to avoid false disappearances. A fresh baseline has been saved.
rpt_drift_new_pairs: New App-to-App Pairs (not seen last period)
rpt_drift_noise_filtered: Noise pairs (ICMP, port 0, ephemeral ports ≥49152) are excluded from drift comparison.
rpt_drift_unlabeled_collapsed: {n} (unlabeled) → (unlabeled) pairs collapsed into this summary line.
rpt_fa_subnote: One consolidated view: each action carries its severity, quantified evidence and affected scope. Rule-check details follow below.
rpt_filter_objects: Source / Destination / Either Side (Labels, IPs, Workloads, IP Lists)
rpt_guidance_audit_mod03_actions: Verify changes are tied to approved tickets; investigate anomalous actors or timing.
rpt_guidance_audit_mod03_how: Each row is a change event with actor, time, and target. Cluster by actor or ruleset to spot patterns.
rpt_guidance_audit_mod03_purpose: Track policy changes and lifecycle events across rulesets.
rpt_guidance_audit_mod03_signals: Off-hours changes, mass deletes, unauthorized actor, policy churn spikes.
rpt_guidance_audit_mod04_actions: Open IR ticket; pull full PCE audit log for chain; verify with VEN telemetry.
rpt_guidance_audit_mod04_how: Correlation chains are time-windowed multi-event sequences with shared actor or workload.
rpt_guidance_audit_mod04_purpose: Correlate suspicious events with policy changes and VEN activity to surface investigation stories.
rpt_guidance_audit_mod04_signals: Auth-failure → policy-change chains; agent tamper → traffic blip; mass-delete → blocked-flow surge.
rpt_guidance_mod02_actions: Author allow rules for legitimate PB flows; investigate blocked flows for attacker activity or misconfiguration.
rpt_guidance_mod02_how: Compare allowed vs PB share. PB does not mean a rule is staged — it means no matching allow exists and only visibility mode is hiding the block.
rpt_guidance_mod02_purpose: Show how observed flows split among allow / block / potentially blocked policy decisions, segmented per profile.
rpt_guidance_mod02_signals: A high potentially_blocked share means coverage gaps. A high blocked share signals attempted disallowed traffic.
rpt_guidance_mod03_actions: Build candidate allow rules; or accept default-deny impact; or investigate as suspicious.
rpt_guidance_mod03_how: Each row is a label-pair / port that lacks coverage. Higher volume + critical destination = higher remediation priority.
rpt_guidance_mod03_purpose: Surface flows with no matching allow rule that would be blocked under default-deny enforcement.
rpt_guidance_mod03_signals: High count of PB uncovered flows; flows targeting crown-jewel labels; flows from unmanaged sources.
rpt_guidance_mod04_actions: Quarantine or restrict; convert legitimate admin flows into named allow rules; investigate the rest.
rpt_guidance_mod04_how: Each row is a workload pair using a high-risk port. Flag if not in your allowed admin-tooling baseline.
rpt_guidance_mod04_purpose: Identify exposure to ransomware-relevant ports and protocols (SMB/RDP/SSH/etc).
rpt_guidance_mod04_signals: Internet-facing or unmanaged sources reaching critical-port destinations.
rpt_guidance_mod07_actions: Convert legitimate cells into label-based allow rules; investigate unexpected high-volume crossings.
rpt_guidance_mod07_how: Read along rows = source group, columns = destination group. Cell intensity reflects flow volume.
rpt_guidance_mod07_purpose: Map source-label → destination-label → service crossings to expose dependency structure.
rpt_guidance_mod07_signals: Unexpected env crossings (prod ↔ dev), unknown labels, app pairs lacking justification.
rpt_guidance_mod08_actions: Install VEN where feasible; document and accept exceptions; tighten boundary rules around unmanaged peers.
rpt_guidance_mod08_how: Each row is an unmanaged IP and its observed peers, ports, and label hits.
rpt_guidance_mod08_purpose: List unmanaged hosts (no VEN) participating in observed flows.
rpt_guidance_mod08_signals: Unmanaged sources accessing crown-jewel apps; high-volume unmanaged egress; unmanaged peers in critical environments.
rpt_guidance_mod13_actions: Convert PB into named allows; resolve unmanaged dependencies; then move app to selective enforcement.
rpt_guidance_mod13_how: Higher PB count = more flows that would be blocked. Higher allowed share with named rules = more ready.
rpt_guidance_mod13_purpose: Estimate enforcement readiness per app/env: how much would shifting to default-deny break vs protect.
rpt_guidance_mod13_signals: Low ready-to-enforce share; high PB exposure; unmanaged dependencies blocking enforcement.
rpt_guidance_mod15_actions: Cut the highest-impact hops via allow rules or boundary deny; isolate crown jewels.
rpt_guidance_mod15_how: Path length, port mix, and crossings between sensitive labels indicate blast-radius risk.
rpt_guidance_mod15_purpose: Show east-west paths an attacker could traverse after initial foothold.
rpt_guidance_mod15_signals: Long chains spanning environments; PB-heavy hops; risky-port chains (SMB/RDP).
rpt_guidance_mod_change_impact_actions: Investigate regressions; document improvements; correlate with audit policy changes.
rpt_guidance_mod_change_impact_how: Each KPI shows previous vs current and direction (improved / regressed / unchanged).
rpt_guidance_mod_change_impact_purpose: Show how key risk metrics changed since the previous report run.
rpt_guidance_mod_change_impact_signals: Big regressions in PB exposure, lateral paths; or improvements after recent policy changes.
rpt_guidance_mod_draft_actions_actions: Review the active allow rule and the override-deny boundary; tighten or remove as needed.
rpt_guidance_mod_draft_actions_how: Override Deny: current policy allows but draft would block. Allowed Across Boundary: flows crossing environment boundaries.
rpt_guidance_mod_draft_actions_purpose: Surface flows where draft policy decisions need human review or remediation.
rpt_guidance_mod_draft_actions_signals: Any override_deny or allowed_across_boundary flows — these represent policy intent gaps.
rpt_guidance_mod_drift_actions: Verify each new pair with the app owner; investigate unexpected new paths to databases or identity services first; confirm disappeared pairs are intentional decommissions.
rpt_guidance_mod_drift_how: New pairs are sorted by connection volume. '(unlabeled)' means the endpoint had no App Label in that period.
rpt_guidance_mod_drift_purpose: Detect changes in the app-to-app connection baseline between report runs — the earliest signal of intrusion, shadow change, or drift.
rpt_guidance_mod_drift_signals: New pairs touching databases, identity infrastructure, or high-risk Ports; large numbers of disappeared pairs after a policy change.
rpt_guidance_mod_ringfence_actions: Author candidate allows; add boundary deny for cross-env exceptions; resolve unmanaged dependencies.
rpt_guidance_mod_ringfence_how: Each candidate rule is a src_label → dst_label → port triple ready for review.
rpt_guidance_mod_ringfence_purpose: Map per-app dependencies and propose label-based ringfence rules.
rpt_guidance_mod_ringfence_signals: High cross-app or cross-env flows lacking allow rules; unknown labels in dependencies.
rpt_guidance_mod_vuln_actions: Patch the top reachable Critical/High assets first; where patching lags, contain them with Policy that cuts the inbound east-west paths.
rpt_guidance_mod_vuln_how: Sorted by severity, then CVSS, then inbound-source breadth — the row at the top is both dangerous and broadly reachable.
rpt_guidance_mod_vuln_purpose: Align patch priority with real reachability: a vulnerable host only matters when non-blocked traffic can actually reach it east-west.
rpt_guidance_mod_vuln_signals: Critical CVEs on assets with many distinct Inbound Sources; high-CVSS rows still reachable across application boundaries.
rpt_guidance_pu_mod03_actions: Disable then remove after a verification period; or replace with tighter rules if needed.
rpt_guidance_pu_mod03_how: Each row is a rule with last-hit timestamp and hit count over the query window.
rpt_guidance_pu_mod03_purpose: Identify unused (zero-hit) policy rules that are candidates for cleanup.
rpt_guidance_pu_mod03_signals: Rules unused for >30 days; broad-scope rules with zero hits (over-permissive but ineffective).
rpt_guidance_pu_mod04_actions: Tighten rule scope; remove redundant denies; investigate bypasses.
rpt_guidance_pu_mod04_how: Effective ratio = blocked flows matching deny scope / total flows in scope.
rpt_guidance_pu_mod04_purpose: Quantify how effective deny rules are: what they actually blocked vs intended scope.
rpt_guidance_pu_mod04_signals: Deny rules with zero hits (possibly redundant); deny rules being bypassed (low effective ratio).
rpt_guidance_ven_lost_heartbeat_actions: Check VEN connectivity to PCE; restart VEN service; escalate if critical workload affected.
rpt_guidance_ven_lost_heartbeat_how: Lost <24h: likely transient. Lost 24-48h: requires investigation. Duration and criticality determine urgency.
rpt_guidance_ven_lost_heartbeat_purpose: Identify VENs that lost contact recently but may still be recoverable.
rpt_guidance_ven_lost_heartbeat_signals: Multiple VENs lost in the same time window; critical workloads affected; recurring pattern.
rpt_guidance_ven_offline_actions: Restart VEN or re-enroll; if persistent, flag the workload as unmanaged in policy planning.
rpt_guidance_ven_offline_how: Offline = no contact > heartbeat threshold. Lost <24h is recoverable; >48h needs intervention.
rpt_guidance_ven_offline_purpose: List VENs that are offline or have lost recent heartbeat — these are segmentation blind spots.
rpt_guidance_ven_offline_signals: Offline VENs on critical workloads; long lost-heartbeat duration; clustered offline failures.
rpt_mod02_minor_note: Folded decisions below 1% of total: {names}. Full detail remains in the data export.
rpt_mod03_rec_cross_app: Cross-app flow: add a rule-set entry for this src_app → dst_app communication.
rpt_mod03_rec_intra_app: Intra-app flow: add an intra-scope rule to allow traffic within the same application.
rpt_mod03_rec_unmanaged_source: Unmanaged source host: onboard to PCE or apply explicit deny / allow rule.
rpt_mod13_col_guide_enforcement: % of workloads in selective or full enforcement (not visibility-only or idle)
rpt_mod13_col_guide_factor: Factor — the aspect of enforcement readiness being measured
rpt_mod13_col_guide_policy: % of flows matched by an allow policy (higher = fewer unprotected paths)
rpt_mod13_col_guide_ratio: Ratio % — the underlying measurement (e.g. 60% of flows are policy-covered)
rpt_mod13_col_guide_remote: % of remote-access flows (RDP/SSH/VNC) that have a matching allow policy
rpt_mod13_col_guide_ringfence: % of apps with app-boundary ringfence policies in place
rpt_mod13_col_guide_score: Score — points earned for this factor (= Weight × Ratio ÷ 100); all factors sum to the total
rpt_mod13_col_guide_staged: % of staged flows (rules written, not yet enforced) that are already covered; shows how much of the backlog is resolved
rpt_mod13_col_guide_weight: Weight — how much this factor contributes to the 100-point total score (e.g. 35 = 35%)
rpt_mod13_err_no_app_env: No app/env records derived from traffic
rpt_mod_change_impact_no_kpi: No KPI data available for change comparison.
rpt_mod_ringfence_no_labels: No app labels available for ringfence analysis.
rpt_no_findings_detail: No security findings were triggered for this dataset. This may indicate good policy coverage or thresholds that need tuning.
rpt_pb_explainer: Traffic observed in visibility mode without a matching allow rule. Would be blocked once selective/full enforcement is enabled.
rpt_pdf_static_note: Static PDF summary. Use HTML or XLSX for full localized detail.
rpt_policy_diff_attribution_note: Attribution shows the most recent matching audit event within the report window; changes outside the window are not attributed.
rpt_policy_diff_report_title: Illumio Policy Diff Report (DRAFT vs ACTIVE)
rpt_pu_caveat_body: Rules with zero traffic hits in the analysed period may still have older hits outside the retention window. Review carefully before removal.
rpt_pu_draft_pd_empty: No potentially-blocking draft decisions detected in the queried period. This may mean all visibility-mode workloads are already policy-covered, or that draft policy decision data is unavailable for this PCE.
rpt_pu_draft_pd_intro: Comprehensive view of draft policy decision impacts: flows at risk of being blocked in stricter enforcement modes, draft policy anomalies such as Override Deny conflicts and Allowed Across Boundary patterns, and flows currently unruled that now have a decision in draft. Review these before switching to Selective enforcement.
rpt_pu_exec_note_pending: {pending} async queries were still pending at timeout.
rpt_pu_footer: Illumio PCE Ops Policy Usage Report
rpt_pu_no_deny: No deny rules found in the active policy.
rpt_pu_no_hit_rules: No rules were hit during this period.
rpt_pu_no_unused_rules: All rules had traffic hits; no unused rules found.
rpt_pu_override_deny_note: these take highest priority and bypass all other rules. Review for correctness.
rpt_pu_unused_caveat: Rules listed here had no observed traffic hits in the selected lookback window. This does not automatically mean the rules are safe to remove because the PCE traffic retention window, low-frequency workloads, or exceptional failover paths may hide valid usage.
rpt_pu_unused_truncated: Showing first 50 of {count} unused rules — full list in CSV/XLSX export.
rpt_readiness_subnote: Answers one question: which app (env) can safely move to enforcement next, and what is blocking the rest. Scores come from the same mod13 analysis as the Security report's readiness chapter.
rpt_readiness_trend_first_run: First run — trend deltas will appear from the next report onward.
rpt_rhc_pulling: Generating native rule hit count report on PCE...
rpt_rhc_html_saved: Rule Hit Count HTML report saved: {path}
rpt_rhc_csv_saved: Rule Hit Count CSV raw data saved: {path}
rpt_rhc_sec_cleanup: Cleanup Candidates (enabled, unused or no hit for {days}+ days)
rpt_rhc_note_semantics: Hit counts are VEN-measured native data. Only Active rules are counted; Essential Rules are excluded.
rpt_rhc_note_optimization: PCE rule optimization may increment merged rules together (counts can overestimate). Each flow attributes at most 100 rules.
rpt_rhc_note_retention: Counts are retained for 90 days; last-hit timestamps are kept permanently.
rpt_rhc_note_enrich_failed: Rule detail enrichment failed — consumer/provider/service columns are empty.
rpt_rhc_note_csv_window: Data window taken from the imported CSV's Start/End Date columns.
cli_rhc_not_enabled: Native Rule Hit Count is not available (state: {state}; {detail}). Use the Policy Usage report for traffic-approximated counts, or enable the native feature.
cli_rhc_not_enabled_intro: The native Rule Hit Count feature is not fully enabled on this PCE.
cli_rhc_provision_warning: WARNING: enabling writes draft firewall_settings and PROVISIONS the change to production policy. VENs older than 23.2.30 will not report.
cli_rhc_confirm_enable: Enable the native Rule Hit Count feature now?
cli_rhc_enable_declined: Enablement declined. You can use the Policy Usage report instead.
cli_rhc_scope_pick_title: Pick labels for the enablement scope
cli_rhc_no_labels_selected: No labels selected — not enabling. Re-run and pick at least one label, or choose 'All VENs' instead.
cli_rhc_ven_delay_note: VENs need time to start reporting — generate the report again later.
cli_rhc_pull_timeout: The PCE has not finished preparing the Rule Hit Count report yet. Please retry later.
rpt_rule_B001_desc: {n_total} non-blocked flows on critical lateral movement ports: {named_ports}. {risk_summary} Flow breakdown — same-subnet: {n_same_subnet}, cross-subnet: {n_cross_subnet}, cross-env: {n_cross_env}, explicitly allowed: {n_allowed}, test-mode only: {n_pb}.
rpt_rule_B001_risk_cross_env: {n_cross_env} flows cross environment boundaries ({n_cross_env_allowed} explicitly allowed, {n_cross_env_pb} potentially_blocked) — active lateral movement path between security domains.
rpt_rule_B001_risk_cross_subnet_allowed: {n_cross_subnet} flows are cross-subnet ({n_allowed} explicitly allowed). Same-subnet flows: {n_same_subnet} (may be legitimate admin).
rpt_rule_B001_risk_cross_subnet_pb: {n_cross_subnet} cross-subnet flows have no allow rule (potentially_blocked) — traffic flows because VEN is in test mode; enforce to activate default-deny.
rpt_rule_B001_risk_same_subnet_allowed: All {n_total} flows are within the same /24 subnet ({n_allowed} allowed, {n_pb} test-mode). Likely Windows administration traffic, but source scope should be verified.
rpt_rule_B001_risk_same_subnet_pb: All {n_total} flows are within the same /24 subnet and in test mode only. Traffic is likely routine Windows admin; block is not active but risk is limited.
rpt_rule_B002_desc: {n_flows} flows on high-risk remote access ports are explicitly allowed: {named}. These flows span {unique_src} unique source IPs → {unique_dst} unique destinations. Remote access tools are the #1 initial access vector for ransomware operators (e.g., Conti, LockBit) who exploit exposed VNC/TeamViewer to gain GUI access without triggering endpoint alerts.
rpt_rule_B003_desc: {n_flows} flows on medium-risk ports are in 'potentially_blocked' state (workloads still in test/visibility mode): {named}. This affects approximately {unique_wl} workload IPs. While these flows would be blocked in enforced mode, they represent real network paths that ransomware could exploit if enforcement is delayed.
rpt_rule_B004_desc: {total} flows originated from unmanaged sources (threshold: {threshold}), targeting {unique_dst} unique managed destinations on ports {top_ports_list}. Top unmanaged source IPs: {top_src_ips_list}. Unmanaged hosts bypass Illumio policy enforcement — any traffic they send cannot be micro-segmented, creating blind spots in your security posture.
rpt_rule_B005_desc: Policy coverage is only {coverage_pct}% — out of {total} total flows, only {allowed} are explicitly allowed by rules, while {blocked} are blocked and {pb} are potentially blocked (test mode). Low coverage means most traffic is flowing without explicit policy authorization, making it impossible to distinguish legitimate traffic from attacker movement.
rpt_rule_B006_desc: {n_high_src} source IPs each connected to >{threshold} unique destinations via lateral movement ports, totalling {total_lateral} flows ({n_allowed_flows} explicitly allowed, {n_pb_flows} potentially_blocked). Top offenders: {top_offenders}. This fan-out pattern is a strong indicator of host-hopping.
rpt_rule_B007_desc: {n_users} user accounts each reached >{threshold} unique destination IPs. Top accounts: {top_accounts} (reaching {top_counts} destinations). Ports used: {top_ports_list}. Normal users typically access a small, predictable set of servers. A single account reaching many destinations may indicate credential theft, automated scanning, or data exfiltration via compromised credentials.
rpt_rule_B008_desc: {n_anomalies} flows exceed the {percentile}th percentile threshold ({threshold_bytes}), transferring a combined {total_anomaly_bytes}. Top flow: {top_src} → {top_dst} ({top_bytes}). Ports involved: {top_ports_list}. Abnormally large data transfers may indicate data exfiltration, unauthorized backups, or misconfigured replication jobs.
rpt_rule_B009_desc: {n_cross} cross-environment flows detected across {n_envs} environments (threshold: {threshold}). Top environment pairs: {top_pairs_str}. Ports: {top_ports_list}. Cross-environment traffic is expected for some services (monitoring, DNS, NTP) but should be explicitly authorized. Uncontrolled cross-env flows break environment isolation and can enable pivot attacks from lower to higher environments.
rpt_rule_L001_desc: {n_flows} flows detected on cleartext protocols (Telnet:{telnet_count}, FTP:{ftp_count}). {n_allowed} of these are explicitly allowed.
rpt_rule_L002_desc: {n_flows} flows on broadcast/discovery protocols are not blocked: {named}. These protocols enable Responder-based NTLMv2 hash harvesting.
rpt_rule_L003_desc: Database ports are reachable from {n_src_apps} unique source applications: {named_ports}. {n_wide} database endpoints are reachable from >{threshold} distinct app tiers.
rpt_rule_L003_name: Database Port Accessible from Many App Tiers
rpt_rule_L004_desc: {n_flows} allowed database flows cross environment boundaries. Top pairs: {top_pairs_str}.
rpt_rule_L005_desc: Identity infrastructure ports (Kerberos/LDAP) are reachable from {unique_src_apps} distinct source apps ({unique_src_apps_allowed} via explicit allow rules, {unique_src_apps_pb} potentially_blocked): {named}. Excessive LDAP/Kerberos reach enables domain enumeration and ticket attacks.
rpt_rule_L006_desc: {n_high_risk} application nodes can reach ≥{threshold} other apps via allowed lateral-port connections (pivoting chains). Top pivot points: {top_pivots_str}.
rpt_rule_L007_desc: {n_flows} flows from {unique_src} unmanaged hosts targeting database/identity/management ports: {named_ports}. Top targets: {top_dst}.
rpt_rule_L008_desc: {n_flows} flows on lateral/critical ports are 'potentially_blocked' ({unique_src} sources → {unique_dst} destinations). These paths are currently traversable because workloads are not fully enforced. Top ports: {top_ports}.
rpt_rule_L008_name: Lateral Ports in Test Mode — Policy Not Enforced
rpt_rule_L009_desc: Managed workloads transferred {total_bytes} to {unique_dst} unmanaged destinations. Top source apps: {top_apps_fmt}.
rpt_rule_L009_name: Data Exfiltration Pattern — Outbound to Unmanaged
rpt_rule_L010_desc: {n_flows} allowed flows use lateral/management ports across environment boundaries. Environment isolation is broken. Top environment pairs: {top_pairs_str}.
rpt_rule_L010_name: Cross-Environment Lateral Port Access — Boundary Break
rpt_rule_b003_desc: Detect attempted broadly-scoped allow rules that conflict with least-privilege baselines.
rpt_rule_l008_desc: Detect lateral movement candidates: chains crossing trust boundaries via risky ports.
rpt_rwp_intro: PCE-native ransomware posture: each VEN's exposure severity, protection coverage, and the high-risk ports actually listening with their owning process.
rpt_tr_anomalies_note: Flows whose bytes-per-connection ratio exceeds the dataset's P95 threshold, excluding one-off connections.
rpt_tr_anomalies_subnote: Flows whose bytes-per-connection exceed P95 (only where connection count > 1); candidates for large transfers or exfiltration.
rpt_tr_audit_flags_subnote: Audit Flags lists traffic that is Allowed but still worth a human review.
rpt_tr_bandwidth_subnote: Start from total volume and peak bandwidth to size overall data movement, then drill into which flows to inspect first.
rpt_tr_col_factor_desc: the aspect of enforcement readiness being measured
rpt_tr_col_ratio_desc: the underlying measurement (e.g. 60% of flows are policy-covered)
rpt_tr_col_score_desc: points earned for this factor (= Weight × Ratio ÷ 100); all factors sum to the total
rpt_tr_col_weight_desc: how much this factor contributes to the 100-point total score (e.g. 35 = 35%)
rpt_tr_distribution_subnote: Distribution tables are mainly for shape — good for spotting concentration around a single Service or sudden spikes in a protocol.
rpt_tr_exposed_ports_merged_subnote: One view per port: connection volume, destination apps and the unmanaged sources driving it.
rpt_tr_footer: Illumio PCE Ops Traffic Flow Report
rpt_tr_host_exposure_note: Destination hosts ranked by the number of distinct risk ports contacted across all policy decisions.
rpt_tr_host_exposure_subnote: Destination hosts ranked by number of risk-Port touches, across all decisions including blocks.
rpt_tr_investigation_desc: The following destination hosts have allowed traffic on critical or high-risk ports. Verify whether this is expected, or block the traffic.
rpt_tr_investigation_note: The following destination hosts have Allowed traffic on critical or high-risk Ports. Verify or consider blocking.
rpt_tr_lateral_intro: Covers all lateral-movement analysis including IP-level host connection patterns and App(Env)-level graph risk scoring.
rpt_tr_lateral_talkers_subnote: IP Top Talkers finds the hosts most active in lateral traffic, handy for checking whether they match known admin nodes.
rpt_tr_maturity_subnote: How to read: the maturity score (0-100) is the weighted sum of five dimensions — enforcement coverage 40, policy coverage 25, lateral-movement control 15, managed-asset ratio 10, risk-port control 10. Each bar shows points earned vs. the dimension's weight; higher is better.
rpt_tr_mod01_intro: This summary frames traffic scale, Policy coverage, and observation period so the rest of the report has context.
rpt_tr_mod02_intro: Start with the decision breakdown to see how much traffic is Allowed vs Blocked vs Potentially Blocked.
rpt_tr_mod13_factor_enforcement: % of workloads in selective or full enforcement (not visibility-only or idle)
rpt_tr_mod13_factor_policy: % of flows matched by an allow policy (higher = fewer unprotected paths)
rpt_tr_mod13_factor_remote: % of remote-access flows (RDP/SSH/VNC) that have a matching allow policy
rpt_tr_mod13_factor_ringfence: % of apps with app-boundary ringfence policies in place
rpt_tr_mod13_factor_staged: % of staged flows (rules written, not yet enforced) that are already covered
rpt_tr_no_investigation: No allowed traffic on critical/high-risk ports detected.
rpt_tr_no_investigation_text: ✅ No Allowed traffic on critical/high-risk Ports detected.
rpt_tr_per_port_src_dst_note: Unique Src IPs: distinct source addresses that sent traffic on this port. Unique Dst IPs: distinct destination addresses that received traffic on this port. The same IP may appear in both counts.
rpt_tr_port_coverage_subnote: Per-Port Coverage surfaces which Services already have solid Policy and which still have gaps.
rpt_tr_port_service_gaps_subnote: Ports ranked by uncovered flows, with the destination apps that receive them — one view for both the port gap and the missing service rules.
rpt_tr_readiness_subnote: How to read: readiness (0-100) is the weighted sum of five factors — policy coverage 35, ringfence maturity 20, enforcement mode 20, staged readiness 15, remote-app coverage 10. Higher means safer to tighten enforcement; the factor table below shows where points were lost.
rpt_tr_sec_bandwidth_intro: Review high-volume flows by bandwidth and data volume to identify large backups, batch jobs, or suspected exfiltration.
rpt_tr_sec_distribution_intro: Observe how overall traffic is distributed across Ports and protocols to quickly spot concentration or unexpected highs.
rpt_tr_sec_drift_intro: Compare this period's app-to-app connections against the previous report to spot new paths and disappeared baselines.
rpt_tr_sec_infrastructure_intro: Identify critical nodes and infrastructure roles with large blast radius from application communication patterns.
rpt_tr_infrastructure_subnote: How to read: each app's infrastructure score = provider 45% + consumer 35% + betweenness 20%. Tier-1 (>=80) means highest blast radius — protect first; Tier-2 >=60, Tier-3 >=40, Tier-4 below.
rpt_tr_sec_lateral_intro: Focus on paths, Services, and sources tied to lateral movement to surface spread risk.
rpt_tr_sec_matrix_intro: Observe cross-group communication by Label dimension, useful for surfacing segments that should not interact frequently.
rpt_tr_sec_overview_intro: Start from overall traffic scale, Policy coverage, and top Ports to set a baseline for reading the rest of the report.
rpt_tr_sec_policy_intro: Break down the ratios and details of Allowed, Blocked, and Potentially Blocked to gauge how Policy is actually landing.
rpt_tr_sec_ransomware_intro: Check high-risk Ports, Allowed flows, and host exposure commonly tied to ransomware attack chains.
rpt_tr_sec_readiness_intro: Aggregate multiple signals into a readiness score to help assess whether it is safe to tighten Enforcement.
rpt_tr_sec_uncovered_intro: Focus on traffic not yet covered by effective Policy, helping prioritise which Services and directions to tighten first.
rpt_tr_sec_unmanaged_intro: Inventory traffic involving hosts not managed by VEN; these typically sit outside the visibility and control boundary.
rpt_tr_sec_user_intro: Add user and process context to traffic to judge whether these connections match existing operational patterns.
rpt_tr_sec_vuln_intro: Rank patching by real east-west reachability: which scanned vulnerabilities sit on hosts that non-blocked traffic can actually reach.
rpt_tr_top_ports_subnote: Top Ports shows which Services dominate the environment and helps spot unexpected activity.
rpt_tr_top_processes_subnote: Top Processes narrows down the binaries that initiated connections, separating normal services from background processes worth investigating.
rpt_tr_top_uncovered_subnote: Top uncovered flows highlight where Policy most urgently needs filling, usually by volume or sensitivity.
rpt_tr_top_users_subnote: Top Users identifies which accounts appear most often in this traffic, helping confirm whether behaviour matches existing patterns.
rpt_tr_trend_empty: No previous snapshot — trend will appear from the next report onward.
rpt_tr_unmanaged_subnote: Start with the scale of unmanaged traffic, then drill into which sources are most active and which managed Services they target.
rpt_trend_mismatch_warning: Comparison caveat: {fields} differ from the previous snapshot — deltas may not be like-for-like.
rpt_ven_footer: Illumio PCE Ops VEN Status Report
rpt_ven_sec_sync_intro: VENs that heartbeat normally but whose security policy has not converged: health errors or warnings, stuck in syncing or staged state, stale firewall config, or policy received but not yet applied. Offline VENs are tracked in the sections below.
rpt_ven_online_detail_note: Per-host online detail is available in the XLSX/CSV export.
rpt_ven_sec_lost_today_intro: Lost heartbeat within the last 24 hours — investigate first. These usually map to recent network, agent, or host incidents.
rpt_ven_sec_lost_yest_intro: Lost heartbeat for more than a day but still recent. Confirm root cause before they become stale assets. Useful for tracking mid-term outages.
rpt_ven_sec_offline_intro: Workloads that are not reporting. Check network, agent status, or whether they were decommissioned. Usually the first stop for asset-visibility issues.
rpt_ven_sec_online_intro: Workloads that keep sending heartbeats and can receive Policy updates. Use this list to confirm the healthy VEN inventory and agent versions.
rpt_vuln_summary: {exposed} of {total} vulnerable assets are reachable east-west
rs_engine_needs_draft_pd: Rule requires draft_policy_decision; compute_draft forced on.
rs_engine_skip_draft: [SKIP] {name} (ID:{id}) is Draft-only and cannot be scheduled.
rs_sch_exists_warn: A schedule already exists for this target.
rs_sch_keep_enter: Press Enter to keep the current value.
rs_target_not_found: [SKIP] {name} (ID:{id}) not found on PCE. No action taken.
rule_agent_clone_desc: Cloned VEN agent detected — multiple workloads sharing the same agent identity.
rule_agent_clone_rec: Identify the duplicate workloads and re-pair them with fresh agent identities to restore reliable enforcement and reporting.
rule_agent_heartbeat_desc: Agent missed multiple heartbeats — the workload is not reporting back to the PCE.
rule_agent_heartbeat_rec: Check workload network connectivity to the PCE, agent service status, and host firewall rules.
rule_agent_offline_desc: Agent is offline — the workload has stopped reporting to the PCE.
rule_agent_offline_rec: Confirm the workload is up, the agent service is running, and the host can reach the PCE on the agent ports.
rule_agent_suspend_desc: VEN agent was suspended on a workload — enforcement is no longer active there.
rule_agent_suspend_rec: Confirm whether the suspension was intentional. If not, re-enable the agent and audit recent admin actions.
rule_agent_tampering_desc: VEN agent on a workload may have been tampered with.
rule_agent_tampering_rec: Verify whether the change was authorized; inspect host integrity (binaries, processes) and recent admin activity on the affected workload.
rule_api_auth_failed_rec: Identify the calling client (IP, user agent), rotate the API key if it leaked, and audit recent integrations.
rule_api_authz_failed_desc: PCE API authorization failures — caller authenticated but lacked permission for the requested action.
rule_api_authz_failed_rec: Audit the role/permission of the failing user/API key; verify whether least-privilege adjustments are needed or if this signals lateral access attempts.
rule_api_key_change_desc: A PCE API key was created or deleted.
rule_api_key_change_rec: Verify the change is expected and that any newly created key is stored securely; audit deletion of keys that may still be in use.
rule_auth_settings_change_rec: Confirm changes are expected (SSO, password policy, session timeout) and review who applied them.
rule_b001_rec_critical_cross_env: CRITICAL: Cross-environment flows on critical ports with explicit allow rules detected. Remove or tightly scope these allow rules immediately. No cross-environment traffic should be permitted on lateral movement ports without a documented exception process. Ports involved: {named_ports}.
rule_b001_rec_cross_subnet_allowed: Cross-subnet flows on {named_ports} indicate unscoped management access or potential lateral movement. Restrict allow rules to: (1) authorised jump-host / bastion IPs only, (2) specific management VLAN ranges. Same-subnet flows ({n_same_subnet}) may be legitimate Windows admin — verify source IPs and scope rules accordingly. Apply Illumio rules with explicit source IP / label constraints.
rule_b001_rec_cross_subnet_pb: Cross-subnet flows on critical ports have no allow rule (potentially_blocked) — no policy covers these paths; they flow only because VEN is in test mode. Move destination workloads to selective or full enforcement to activate default-deny.
rule_b001_rec_pb_cross_env: CRITICAL: Cross-environment flows detected with no allow rule (potentially_blocked). No explicit policy permits this traffic — it flows only because workloads are in test mode. Move workloads to enforced mode to activate default-deny and block these paths immediately. Investigate why this cross-env traffic exists on: {named_ports}.
rule_b001_rec_same_subnet_allowed: Same-subnet flows on critical ports are typical for Windows server administration (RDP to nearby servers, SMB file shares, WinRM remote management). Recommended actions: (1) Verify each source IP is an authorised admin system or jump host, (2) Scope Illumio allow rules to specific source IPs rather than allowing all same-subnet access — this prevents lateral movement if any same-subnet host is compromised, (3) Confirm SMB 445 and RPC 135 are not used for general file sharing across workloads.
rule_b001_rec_same_subnet_pb: Same-subnet traffic on critical ports in test mode only. Low immediate risk — verify source IPs are authorised admin systems, then move workloads to enforced mode. Consider scoping allow rules to specific admin IPs rather than subnet-wide.
rule_b002_rec: 1) Verify each allowed flow has a documented business justification. 2) Block TeamViewer (5938) and VNC (5900/5901) unless required for specific admin workflows — replace with MFA-protected jump servers. 3) If NetBIOS (137-139) is allowed, migrate to SMBv3 over 445 with encryption. 4) Create Illumio deny rules for these ports from all non-admin workloads.
rule_b003_rec: 1) Prioritize moving workloads with potentially_blocked flows to enforced mode — start with the highest-risk ports (SSH, NFS). 2) Review the 'potentially_blocked' flows to ensure no legitimate traffic will break when enforcement is applied. 3) Create allow rules for verified legitimate traffic before switching to enforced mode. 4) Set a deadline for enforcement — prolonged test mode leaves the network exposed.
rule_b004_rec: 1) Identify each unmanaged source IP — are they network devices, legacy servers, or shadow IT? 2) Deploy VEN agents on servers that should be managed. 3) For network devices that can't run a VEN, use IP lists and enforcement boundaries to control their access. 4) Create explicit deny rules for any unmanaged-to-managed path that isn't required.
rule_b005_rec: 1) Focus first on critical tiers: databases, identity infrastructure (AD/LDAP), and externally-facing workloads. 2) Use Illumio's Explorer to identify top unruled flows and create allow rules for verified traffic. 3) Set a target coverage of >{threshold}% within 30 days. 4) Enable enforcement gradually — start with ring-fencing high-value assets, then expand to general workloads.
rule_b006_rec_allowed: 1) Investigate the top source IPs — check for exploit tools, unexpected SSH sessions, or RDP brute-force. 2) Remove or tightly scope the allow rules driving the high-fan-out flows. 3) Ring-fence high-value assets (databases, domain controllers) to limit blast radius.
rule_b006_rec_pb: 1) Investigate the top source IPs — check for exploit tools, unexpected SSH sessions, or RDP brute-force. 2) Move workloads to enforced mode — no allow rules exist for these paths; default-deny will block them upon enforcement. 3) Ring-fence high-value assets (databases, domain controllers) to limit blast radius.
rule_b007_rec: 1) Cross-reference flagged accounts with HR/IT records — are these admin accounts or regular users? 2) Check authentication logs for failed login attempts or impossible-travel alerts. 3) For admin accounts, enforce just-in-time access and MFA for lateral sessions. 4) Create Illumio user-based rules to limit which destinations each role can access. 5) If compromise is suspected, rotate credentials immediately and isolate the workload.
rule_b008_rec: 1) Verify the top bandwidth consumers — are they backup servers, replication, or database sync? Document expected high-volume flows. 2) For unexplained transfers, check if the source is a compromised workload exfiltrating data to an external or lateral destination. 3) Consider implementing bandwidth-aware rules or QoS policies for known high-volume services. 4) Set up Illumio monitoring alerts for sustained anomalous volume.
rule_b009_rec: 1) Audit each cross-env flow pair — document which ones are expected (e.g., Production monitoring pulling from Staging). 2) Create explicit Illumio rules for approved cross-env paths with specific port restrictions. 3) Block all other cross-env flows, especially database ports (see L004) and admin protocols (SSH, RDP). 4) Use Illumio environment labels consistently to enforce isolation boundaries.
rule_bulk_unpair_desc: Bulk unpair of workloads or agents detected — large-scale enforcement removal.
rule_bulk_unpair_rec: Verify the bulk operation was sanctioned (decommission, environment refresh); investigate immediately if not.
rule_high_blocked_desc: High volume of blocked traffic detected.
rule_high_blocked_rec: Review the top blocked flows and verify whether Policy needs adjustment.
rule_l001_rec: Immediately disable Telnet (port 23) and FTP (ports 20/21). Replace with SSH (22) or SFTP. Cleartext credentials can be captured by any attacker with network access, enabling trivial lateral movement.
rule_l002_rec: Block NetBIOS (137-138), LLMNR (5355), and SSDP (1900) at the segmentation layer. These protocols serve no legitimate role in modern micro-segmented environments and are used almost exclusively for network reconnaissance and credential poisoning attacks (e.g., Responder / Inveigh).
rule_l003_rec: Database ports should only be reachable from their direct application tier (typically 1-2 app labels). Use Illumio rule-sets to restrict db access to approved app labels only. Wide database exposure is the primary post-exploitation data exfiltration path after lateral movement succeeds.
rule_l004_rec: Enforce strict environment isolation for all database ports. No Dev/Test/Staging application should have direct access to Production databases. Use read replicas or API gateways as the cross-environment interface, never direct DB ports.
rule_l005_rec_allowed: Restrict Kerberos (88) and LDAP (389/636) to domain-joined workloads only. Remove or tightly scope allow rules permitting broad identity-port access. No application tier should query LDAP directly — use a service account wrapper.
rule_l005_rec_pb: These flows have no allow rule — move workloads to enforced mode so default-deny blocks them. Before enforcing, create allow rules only for legitimate LDAP consumers (domain-joined workloads). No application tier should query LDAP directly — use a service account wrapper.
rule_l006_rec: Reduce lateral-port connectivity between application tiers using Illumio rule-sets. Each application should only be able to reach its direct dependencies — not transitively reach the entire network. Prioritise isolating the top pivot apps listed in evidence.
rule_l007_rec: Unmanaged hosts should NEVER directly reach database, identity, or Windows management ports. Options: (1) Onboard the hosts to PCE immediately, (2) Add explicit deny rules for unmanaged sources on these ports, (3) Investigate whether these are attacker-controlled hosts or shadow IT. Priority: verify the top source IPs are known/authorised assets.
rule_l008_rec: Move workloads from visibility/test mode to selective or full enforcement. Until enforcement is active, 'potentially_blocked' flows are live attack paths — no allow rule covers them; only enforced mode's default-deny will block them. Check enforcement mode in PCE for the destination apps: {top_apps}.
rule_l009_rec: Investigate all managed-to-unmanaged outbound flows above the threshold. Legitimate outbound traffic (internet APIs, CDN) should be explicitly allowed and scoped to known destination IPs or ranges. Apply an outbound allowlist policy and deny all other external destinations. Top suspicious destinations (by bytes): {top_dst_fmt}.
rule_l010_rec: CRITICAL: Lateral movement ports (SMB 445, RDP 3389, WinRM 5985/5986, RPC 135) must NEVER be allowed across environment boundaries. Implement deny rules at the environment boundary level immediately. This represents an active lateral movement path between environments that an attacker can exploit to escalate from low-security to high-security zones.
rule_login_failed_desc: Repeated PCE Console login failures — possible brute-force or stale credential reuse.
rule_login_failed_rec: Investigate the source IP and account; consider tightening password / MFA policy or temporarily disabling the account if abuse is confirmed.
rule_lost_agent_desc: Previously offline agent has come back online (lost-agent recovery).
rule_lost_agent_rec: Confirm the recovered workload is in expected state and review what caused the original outage.
rule_pce_health_rec: Check PCE service status and network connectivity.
rule_policy_fail_desc: Agent failed to refresh policy from the PCE.
rule_policy_fail_rec: Check workload-to-PCE connectivity, agent logs, and recent policy changes that may have introduced an invalid rule.
rule_policy_provision_desc: A new Security Policy was provisioned (sec_policy.create).
rule_policy_provision_rec: Confirm the provisioning operation was intended; review the workloads_affected count and the operator who authorized it.
rule_r01_desc: Flows currently allowed but a draft deny rule would block them once provisioned.
rule_r01_rec: Review and provision the draft deny rules, or add explicit allow rules before provisioning to avoid unexpected traffic disruption.
rule_r02_desc: Override deny rule present; cannot be overridden by any allow rule.
rule_r02_rec: Override deny rules take precedence over all allow rules. Verify each override deny is intentional before provisioning.
rule_r03_desc: VEN in visibility/test mode and a deny boundary draft exists for this flow.
rule_r03_rec: Move workloads to enforced mode to activate the boundary deny. Flows are currently traversable only because VENs are in test/visibility mode.
rule_r04_desc: Allow rule overrides a regular deny. Verify this is intentional.
rule_r04_rec: Confirm that cross-boundary allow rules are intentional and tightly scoped. Consider whether a more restrictive rule can meet the business requirement.
rule_r05_desc: Aggregated list of workload pairs where reported=allowed but draft suggests block.
rule_r05_rec: Review these workload pairs before provisioning draft rules. Currently-allowed traffic will be blocked once the draft is provisioned.
rule_ruleset_change_desc: A Ruleset was created, updated, or deleted.
rule_ruleset_change_rec: Confirm the change is expected (named change ticket / approved request); review the diff against the prior version.
rule_saved: Rule saved. Press Enter to continue...
rule_sec_rule_change_desc: A Security Rule was created, updated, or deleted.
rule_sec_rule_change_rec: Confirm the rule change matches an approved request; check the diff and re-test policy assumptions for affected workloads.
save_debug_query: Save raw queried data to JSON for debugging? (y/n)

rpt_kf_staged_enforcement_msg: Only {cov:.0f}% of flows are enforced, but {staged:.0f}% are staged (rules ready, pending enforcement).
rpt_kf_staged_enforcement_reco: Move workloads from test/visibility to selective or full enforcement.
rpt_kf_policy_gap_msg: Only {cov:.0f}% of flows are covered by allow policies — true gap is {gap:.0f}%.
rpt_kf_policy_gap_reco: Create segmentation rules for the top uncovered flows.
rpt_action_lock_boundary_ports: Restrict cross-boundary ports to explicit allowlists and remove broad access.
rpt_action_move_to_enforcement: Move scoped workloads from visibility/testing into selective or full enforcement.
rpt_action_define_ringfence_scope: Tighten ringfence scope so only required app-to-app paths are permitted.
rpt_action_review_remote_access_allowlis: Review remote-access flows and keep only approved source-to-destination pairs.
rpt_action_tighten_lateral_policy: Reduce traversable lateral paths and segment high-reachability nodes.
rpt_action_restrict_transit_node_access: This workload acts as a network hub (high reachability or articulation point). Apply stricter east-west policy boundaries to limit lateral movement.
rpt_action_onboard_unmanaged: Onboard unmanaged assets or explicitly isolate their access paths.
rpt_action_review_unused_rulesets: Review high-unused rulesets and narrow policy scope to active dependencies.
rpt_action_resolve_query_failures: Resolve pending/failed usage queries before making policy retirement decisions.
rpt_action_investigate_high_risk_port_hi: Investigate concentration of high-risk port hits and validate least-privilege policy intent.
rpt_action_review_high_impact_provisions: Review high-impact provisions and confirm boundary-sensitive changes were authorized.
rpt_action_harden_auth_channels: Harden authentication channels and investigate repeated failed authentication behavior.
rpt_action_default: Review attack posture evidence and apply least-privilege segmentation.
